name: Databricks
description: This app supports investigation and data manipulation actions in Databricks

parameters.name: run_id
parameters.description: Job run id

parameters.name: run_id
parameters.description: Job run id

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Name of channel

parameters.name: query_id
parameters.description: ID of the query evaluated by the alert

parameters.name: column
parameters.description: Name of column in the query result to compare in alert evaluation

parameters.name: operator
parameters.description: Operator used to compare in alert evaluation

parameters.name: value
parameters.description: Value used to compare in alert evaluation

parameters.name: custom_body
parameters.description: Custom body of alert notification

parameters.name: custom_subject
parameters.description: Custom subject of alert notification

parameters.name: muted
parameters.description: Whether or not the alert is muted

parameters.name: rearm
parameters.description: Number of seconds after being triggered before the alert rearms itself and can be triggered again. If not set, alert will never be triggered again

parameters.name: parent
parameters.description: The identifier of the workspace folder containing the alert. The default is the user's home folder

parameters.name: empty_result_state
parameters.description: State that alert evaluates to when query result is empty

parameters.name: alert_id
parameters.description: ID of the alert to delete

actions.name: List Warehouses
actions.description: List all SQL warehouses for which a user has manager permissions

actions.name: Cancel Query
actions.description: Request that an executing SQL statement be cancelled. Callers must poll for a status of the end state

parameters.name: statement_id
parameters.description: SQL statement id

actions.name: Get Query Status
actions.description: Get status, manifest, and result first chunk of a SQL query

parameters.name: statement_id
parameters.description: SQL statement id

parameters.name: statement
parameters.description: SQL statement to execute

parameters.name: warehouse_id
parameters.description: Warehouse upon which to execute a statement

parameters.name: wait_timeout
parameters.description: The time in seconds the API service will wait for the statement's result. Can be set to 0 or to a value between 5 and 50. When set to 0 the statement will execute in asynchronous mode

parameters.name: on_wait_timeout
parameters.description: When in synchronous mode with wait_timeout > 0s the action taken when the timeout is reached

parameters.name: byte_limit
parameters.description: Applies the given byte limit to the statement's result size

parameters.name: catalog
parameters.description: Sets default catalog for statement execution, similar to USE CATALOG in SQL

parameters.name: schema
parameters.description: Sets default schema for statement execution, similar to USE SCHEMA in SQL.

parameters.name: disposition
parameters.description: The result disposition

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: The result format

parameters.name: notebook_path
parameters.description: The path of the notebook to be run in the Databricks workspace or remote repository

parameters.name: git_source
parameters.description: An optional specification for a remote repository containing the notebook

parameters.name: existing_cluster_id
parameters.description: The ID of an existing cluster to use for all runs of this task (required if new_cluster is unspecified)

parameters.name: new_cluster
parameters.description: An object specifying the details of the new cluster to use for this task (required if existing_cluster_id is unspecified)

parameters.name: libraries
parameters.description: An optional list of libraries to be installed on the cluster that executes the task

parameters.name: timeout_seconds
parameters.description: An optional timeout applied to the job

parameters.name: run_name
parameters.description: An optional name for the run

parameters.name: idempotency_token
parameters.description: An optional token that can be used to guarantee the idempotency of job run requests. If a run with the provided token already exists, the request does not create a new run but returns the info of the existing run instead. If a run with the provided token is deleted, an error is returned

parameters.name: access_control_list
parameters.description: An optional list of permissions to set for the run

parameters.name: start_time
parameters.description: Parameter ignored in this app

parameters.name: end_time
parameters.description: Parameter ignored in this app

parameters.name: container_id
parameters.description: Parameter ignored in this app

parameters.name: container_count
parameters.description: Parameter ignored in this app

parameters.name: artifact_count
parameters.description: Parameter ignored in this app
